title: rumex acetosella var. pyrenaicus
intro: Rumex acetosella var. pyrenaicus, also known as the Pyrenean sorrel or mountain sorrel, is a subspecies of the red sorrel plant native to the Pyrenees mountain range. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves that have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues, fever, and skin irritations. The leaves of Rumex acetosella var. pyrenaicus are rich in vitamins A and C, and have been used to alleviate health issues such as scurvy, anemia, and inflammation, while also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
